tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with a scholarship due Thursday that could significantly help my finances, requiring about three hours to complete. While I have low energy and sleep debt, ignoring this opportunity for a temporary fun activity seems like a poor trade-off given the potential long-term benefit.
Starting the scholarship application now creates slack to handle unexpected friction before Thursday, and delaying the fun activity incurs no cost since it remains available later.
I have completed only 42% of the scholarship application in two hours, meaning I still need to finish the essay and uploads before the deadline.
I made progress on the scholarship but missed a fleeting opportunity for immediate fun; I should capitalize on this momentum while it lasts.
I advanced the scholarship application to 60% in 1.5 hours, confirming that focused work is yielding progress toward the deadline.
I've progressed the scholarship application to 66% after working nearly an hour and a half, confirming that focused effort is effectively moving me toward the deadline.
I've reached 71% on the scholarship application after working 1.4 hours, confirming that my focused effort is effectively closing in on the deadline.
I am at 77% on my scholarship application with three days left, but my energy is depleted and stress remains high despite the progress.
I advanced the scholarship application to 83% in just over an hour, but my zero energy and high stress indicate I cannot sustain this pace without severe consequences.
I advanced the scholarship application to 89%, but with zero energy and high stress, continuing this pace is unsustainable.
I have completed 94% of my scholarship application, but with zero energy and high stress, I must stop working now to avoid burnout.
I advanced the scholarship application to 96%, but my zero energy and high stress confirm I must stop now to prevent burnout.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
